# Tiny tech, big impact : Monitoring air quality with low-cost sensors

June 4, 2024

Have you ever wondered about the air you breathe? From city streets to countryside valleys, air quality can impact your health. Thanks to advancements in technology, monitoring air quality is becoming easier and more accessible than ever before.

Researchers have been monitoring air quality for years, using instruments known as ”regulatory” air monitors. While very accurate, these are quite expensive. They are located in 286 sites in 203 communities across Canada and run by Environment and Climate Change Canada as part of the [National Air Pollution Surveillance Program](https://www.canada.ca/en/environment-climate-change/services/air-pollution/monitoring-networks-data/national-air-pollution-program.html).

These regulatory monitoring networks continuously check up on air quality, provide information on air quality trends, support research, and help governments make decisions about how to best manage air pollution. They are also used to calculate the Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) in Canada, a vital tool designed to help people understand what the air quality around them means to their health.

![White electronic device with a screen that measures surrounding air quality, clipped to a wooden fence.](/site/science/sites/default/files/image/1236-sensor-capteur-1-555x402.jpg)

*Low-cost sensors such as this one can provide immediate information on air quality and other environmental factors.*

Now, low-cost air sensors are changing the game, especially for rural and remote communities. These lower-cost devices allow people to monitor air quality in their immediate surroundings. They offer scientists and governments another way to measure air quality in smaller and less accessible communities to fill gaps in the monitoring network. While they may not be as accurate as the regulatory monitors, they can still provide valuable information, especially in areas where regulatory monitoring stations aren’t available.

Currently, low-cost sensors are used to monitor fine particulate matter (called PM2.5) concentrations, one of the three air pollutants measured as part of the AQHI. Data from outdoor sensors can be accessed through platforms such as the [AQMap](https://aqmap.ca/aqmap/), allowing anyone to easily access information about levels of PM2.5 in their area. Air quality can vary greatly over small areas, so having multiple low-cost sensors displayed together on the AQMap can provide useful information. However, it is important to note that the data from small sensors is not a representation of the full AQHI. The AQHI also includes measurements of other pollutants, in addition to PM2.5. As such, small sensors can help in communities who weren’t able to collect this data in the past, even if it provides less detailed information than the AQHI.

## Harnessing the potential of low-cost sensors

Ryan Kulka, a researcher from Health Canada, is harnessing the potential of low-cost sensors in his work. By using data from these sensors, Ryan and his team are exploring the effects of wildfire smoke and heat on indoor air quality. With the help of their partners, they're also investigating how well ventilation systems in buildings and building envelopes themselves can filter out smoke during wildfires. “Every instrument has its role to play, and we are finding ways to use these low-cost sensors in our research,” says Ryan.

![Inside components of an air quality sensor showing the sensing elements that measure temperature and relative humidity.](/site/science/sites/default/files/image/1236-sensor-capteur-2-555x281.jpg)

*Low-cost sensors use one or more sensing elements to measure and report on air pollutants and environmental factors such as temperature and relative humidity.*

With the help of low-cost sensors, researchers like Ryan are finding innovative ways to protect public health and improve air quality from coast to coast to coast. These sensors are available for purchase by provinces, territories, organizations, and individuals.

Low-cost air sensors are revolutionizing the way we monitor air quality. They empower individuals and communities to better understand their environment and contribute to a healthier future for all Canadians.

# A cost-effective IoT-based intelligent indoor air quality monitoring

A cost-effective IoT-based intelligent indoor air quality monitoring

**Sanithu Heengama (1) and Kosala Heengama (1)**

(1) Colombo International School, Colombo, Sri Lanka

Sep 05, 2023

![Cover photo for A cost-effective IoT-based intelligent indoor air quality monitoring](https://emerginginvestigators.org/rails/active_storage/representations/proxy/eyJfcmFpbHMiOnsibWVzc2FnZSI6IkJBaHBBdjRPIiwiZXhwIjpudWxsLCJwdXIiOiJibG9iX2lkIn19--5fc34953e4b9388b4c113aca5ba7395c8130d0a7/eyJfcmFpbHMiOnsibWVzc2FnZSI6IkJBaDdCem9MWm05eWJXRjBTU0lJY0c1bkJqb0dSVlE2QzNKbGMybDZaVWtpRFRZd01IZzJNREErQmpzR1ZBPT0iLCJleHAiOm51bGwsInB1ciI6InZhcmlhdGlvbiJ9fQ==--33b2b080106a274a4ca568f8742d366d42f20c14/Presentation4.png)

Air quality monitoring has become a critical issue for human beings as it is related to living quality. Poor air quality harms human health, worsening respiratory diseases, increasing heart risks, impacting brain health, and endangering vulnerable populations. Various countries legislate air quality norms related to air monitoring and measurement standards. Nonetheless, most of the monitoring stations for air pollution are set up outdoors and monitor sources of air pollution and greenhouse gases in larger regions, neglecting the importance of indoor air quality (IAQ). Thus, monitoring the IAQ for better healthcare has become necessary. However, low-cost sensors are often associated with design compromises that hamper data reliability. Although scientists have extensively studied IAQ, the practical application of low-cost devices in indoor environments has yet to be investigated. We explored the effectiveness of a low- cost IAQ experimental monitoring device by studying the quality of the results gathered from the reference monitor unit. We hypothesized that the advanced technology and sensors would provide accurate and valid data to make the low-cost IAQ monitor feasible for widespread usage. Our goal was to provide an intelligent, movable, and cost-effective indoor air quality monitoring unit which has been designed to simulate users' indoor routes and detect air quality in real-time. Since, the main factor affecting the accuracy and validity of the measurements results from its orthogonal design. Our results revealed a low-cost monitoring device can be used with efficient design and by using sensors that are highly accurate which are low-cost for accurate and valid readings.

This article has been tagged with:

***J Emerg Invest***

ISSN 2638-0870

# The Potential of Low-Cost Sensors for Comprehensive Air Quality Management

Low-cost air quality sensors are revolutionizing environmental monitoring, offering precision and accessibility. Dive into how these tools are paving the way for proactive, comprehensive air quality management.

## **Unlocking the secrets of air quality with low-cost air pollution sensors**

**TL:DR:** Air quality isn't just another environmental buzzword; it's a critical aspect of our daily lives that affects health, productivity, and the planet. Enter low-cost air quality sensors, a game-changer in the fight for a cleaner environment. These devices are transforming how we track, analyze, and tackle air pollution, making it possible for anyone, anywhere, to get real-time data about the air they breathe.Â

This isn't about fancy tech for the sake of it; it's about empowering communities, shaping policies, and driving action towards better air quality. By deploying air pollution sensors, we're not just monitoring the air; we're unlocking the secrets to healthier communities and a sustainable future. In todayâs blog, weâll cover:Â

## **The science behind low-cost air quality sensors**

Low-cost air quality sensors pack a powerful technological punch, enabling widespread monitoring of air pollution with surprising precision and flexibility. These devices utilize a range of sensors to detect various pollutants, including [particulate matter (PM2.5 and PM10)](https://www.clarity.io/blog/air-quality-measurements-series-particulate-matter), [nitrogen dioxide](https://www.clarity.io/blog/air-quality-measurements-series-nitrogen-dioxide), sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide, and [ozone](https://www.clarity.io/blog/air-quality-measurements-series-ozone), among others. Here's a closer look at how they work and their significance in air quality management:

â

The evolution of low-cost air quality sensors represents a significant leap forward in environmental technology. By making air quality monitoring more accessible and affordable, these sensors are democratizing the fight against pollution. As they continue to improve in accuracy and reliability, their potential to support comprehensive air quality management grows, marking a new era in environmental health and protection.

## **Real-world applications: from homes to cities**

Low-cost air quality sensors are making a big difference in how we understand and manage air pollution, from the comfort of our homes to the complexity of city-wide networks, like those that Clarity specializes in. These sensors are used in a variety of ways, showing just how versatile this technology can be.

Clarity's [air quality management services](https://www.clarity.io/air-quality-monitoring-solution) support these applications by providing accurate, reliable data from low-cost sensors. This technology empowers communities, researchers, and policymakers to make informed decisions, aligning with Clarity's vision of a world where air quality management is proactive, informed, and inclusive.Â

Clarity is at the forefront of using [low-cost sensor technology](https://www.clarity.io/products/clarity-node-s) to address air quality challenges, demonstrating the practical impact and potential of these sensors in real-world settings. For more insights into the diverse applications of these sensors, explore the myriad use cases for low-cost air quality sensors on [Clarity's blog](https://www.clarity.io/blog/the-myriad-use-cases-for-low-cost-air-quality-sensors)*.*

## **Accuracy and data management with low-cost sensors**

Despite the promising applications, users of low-cost air quality sensors face two main challenges: ensuring the accuracy of the data and effectively managing the vast amounts of information collected. These hurdles can seem daunting, but with the right tools and strategies, they're entirely surmountable.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f6bb9b58b09db262f667813/665a4998db7a3447c2f6ed16_AD_4nXeWAv1OzHSNxCjQ4kJJoFDLPh4RgBFwou9gIVLgNKhoqFsBy0MNrb9SIj28L63CW5J8kUwiKuImfzyN5uznamS4GuayAfLqvFXQYg0ZCoCUIq4AQXeMlChW4ynxeohW4LUXHuMpMD7b9v5nO90dZksuMuDH.jpeg)

By tackling these challenges head-on, Clarity empowers users to harness the full potential of low-cost air quality sensors. Our solutions not only facilitate accurate and efficient air quality monitoring but also pave the way for comprehensive air quality management. Through our commitment to data integrity and ease of use, we're helping communities, researchers, and policymakers around the globe to make informed decisions for a healthier future.

## **Community engagement and environmental justice**

A clean environment for all is at the core of Clarity's mission. By leveraging low-cost air quality sensors, communities gain a powerful tool to monitor their environment, advocate for change, and participate in the decision-making process regarding air quality management. This democratization of data empowers individuals and communities, especially in areas disproportionately affected by pollution, to take action towards environmental justice.

By putting the power of air quality monitoring into the hands of the people, Clarity is not just offering a technological solution; it's fostering a movement toward greater environmental justice. Through community engagement and the provision of reliable, easy-to-use sensors, Clarity is helping to build a foundation for lasting change, ensuring that every community has the tools they need to advocate for a healthier, more sustainable future.

## **The future of air quality monitoring**

Looking ahead, the potential for advancements in low-cost sensor technology is vast and exciting. As these sensors become even more sophisticated and user-friendly, we're looking at a future where comprehensive air quality management is within everyone's reach. This isn't just about incremental changes; we're talking about a revolution in how we understand, interact with, and ultimately improve the air around us.

This future is not just about technology; it's about creating healthier communities and a more sustainable world. With each advancement in sensor technology, we move closer to a future where everyone has access to the information they need to protect their health and the environment. The journey toward better air quality management through innovative solutions like those offered by [Clarity](https://www.clarity.io/) is just beginning, and the possibilities are as limitless as our commitment to a cleaner, healthier planet.

## **Taking action: how to get started with low-cost sensors**

Through this exploration of low-cost air quality sensors, we've highlighted their vital role in enhancing our understanding and management of air pollution. These devices offer a powerful combination of affordability, precision, and versatility, making air quality monitoring accessible to all. From empowering individuals to make healthier choices about their indoor environments to enabling cities to devise smarter urban planning strategies, low-cost sensors are at the forefront of the fight for cleaner air.

The journey towards better air quality starts with taking informed steps. Whether you're part of a community group eager to advocate for environmental justice, a researcher conducting in-depth studies, or a public health official looking to safeguard your community, low-cost sensors provide the data needed to drive meaningful change. The insights gathered can shape policies, foster public awareness, and ultimately lead to healthier living spaces.

Now is the time to harness the potential of low-cost air quality sensors. Embrace the opportunity to be part of a global movement towards environmental stewardship and proactive air quality management. If you're ready to take the next step and explore air quality monitoring solutions tailored to your needs, Clarity is here to guide you.

[Build your Solution](https://www.clarity.io/build-your-solution) with Clarity today. Let us help you select the right air quality solution, empowering you to make informed decisions for a cleaner, healthier future. We can transform the way we understand and respond to air pollution.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Smart sensors are cost-effective, often priced below $1,000, making them accessible for widespread deployment in urban and rural areas, thus promoting community-based air quality monitoring.
These sensors require minimal upkeep compared to traditional monitoring systems, allowing for continuous operation without frequent service interruptions, thus ensuring reliable data collection.
Smart sensors can be easily scaled across various locations, enabling comprehensive air quality networks that adapt to community needs while enhancing pollution tracking capabilities.
